review_summary: The transcript supports two questions on Flamingo training and few-shot multimodal reasoning without needing a clip.

question_bank.question: Why does Flamingo use a masking scheme during interleaved image-text training?
choices: So each generated description attends to the correct image while still benefiting from a larger multimodal context
So the model never sees more than one image during training
So it can remove the need for any language-model pretraining
So all images are forced to share the same caption representation
question_bank.explanation: Interleaved sequences provide rich context, but the model still needs to ground each description in the right image. The masking scheme preserves that alignment.

question_bank.question: What does in-context visual learning mean in this setting?
choices: Providing example image-text or image-QA pairs so the model continues the same pattern on a new image
Finetuning the whole multimodal model on a labeled dataset before every query
Restricting the model to answer only yes-or-no visual questions
Replacing image inputs with nearest-neighbor retrieval results
question_bank.explanation: The model can infer the task from examples shown in the prompt, then apply that pattern to a new image without changing its weights.
